description: CarFlip detection keyword library for Facebook Marketplace seller descriptions. Synthesized from 5 AI analyses + 80 real listings.

_philosophy.bias: Under-flag is better than over-flag. False positives lose user trust faster than false negatives.
_philosophy.normal_wear: Tires, brakes, alignment, oil leaks, struts, AC service, batteries are EXPECTED flip costs — NEVER flag them.
_philosophy.context_matters: Same keyword can be RED, YELLOW, or GREEN depending on negation/repair language nearby.

severity_levels.RED: DIG DEEPER — Flip-critical issue, likely changes profit math
severity_levels.YELLOW: CAUTION — Verify before committing, ask seller specific question
severity_levels.GREEN: NORMAL — Bilinen flip maliyeti, ignored in verdict
severity_levels.POSITIVE: Good signal, raises confidence in deal
severity_levels.DEALER_AD: This is a dealer listing, not a private flip target

title_critical.cost_estimate: Title brand cuts retail resale value 30-50%
title_critical.verify_question: Can you show me the title?

title_caution.cost_estimate: Rebuilt title alone: resale value drops 20-30%, financing difficult
title_caution.verify_question: Why was it rebuilt? Can I see the original damage report?
title_caution.note: If car drives fine and is priced for rebuilt status, can still be flipped. Becomes RED if combined with driving symptoms.

engine_critical.verify_question: Is it a top-end tick (cheap) or bottom-end rod knock (expensive)?

transmission_critical.verify_question: When did slipping start? Does it slip cold or hot?

accident_severe.cost_estimate: Structural damage: hidden costs $1,500-$5,000+, may fail inspection
accident_severe.verify_question: Has frame been straightened? Any frame damage report?

accident_minor.verify_question: Was airbag deployed? Any frame impact?

history_critical.cost_estimate: TMU/odometer issues: car age unknown, resale crippled
history_critical.verify_question: Why is mileage unknown? Carfax report?

as_is_red.cost_estimate: Parts car / not driveable: only valuable as parts ($500-$1500 in parts)
as_is_red.verify_question: Does it run and drive at all?
english: part out or selling as whole

as_is_caution.cost_estimate: As-is purchase: unknown repair scope, budget extra $1,000-$2,000
as_is_caution.verify_question: What specifically needs work? Cost estimate?
english: drivetrain issues contact for more details
spanish: se vende tal y como está

cel_warning_lights.verify_question: What specific code? Has mechanic scanned it?
cel_warning_lights.note: ALWAYS YELLOW, even if seller explains the code. Independent verification needed.

_per_code_meaning.p0420: Catalytic converter inefficiency - can drive, will fail emissions
_per_code_meaning.p0430: Catalytic converter bank 2 - same as P0420
_per_code_meaning.p0442: EVAP small leak - gas cap loose, usually trivial
_per_code_meaning.p0455: EVAP large leak - gas cap or EVAP system
_per_code_meaning.p0300: Random misfire - engine running rough, ignition system
_per_code_meaning.p0301-4: Cylinder X misfire - spark plug, coil, or compression

inspection_critical.cost_estimate: Cannot drive legally until passes inspection

normal_wear_ignore.cost_estimate: Expected flip cost — already priced in
normal_wear_ignore.note: These are NORMAL WEAR. Do NOT flag. They appear in summary only as 'expected costs to budget'.

positive_replaced.note: When a problem keyword has 'replaced/new/rebuilt' nearby, it BECOMES positive

english: owner is no longer in the country
send to private and i explain
spanish: la dueña ya no se encuentra en el país
el dueño está en el extranjero
tiren al privado y yo les explico

dealer_ad.note: This is a dealer ad pushing financing, not a private flip target. Skip.
spanish: no tienes ssn no hay problema

positive_smog_ca.verify_question: Is the smog certificate recent (CA requires it at point of sale)?

smog_ca_needed.cost_estimate: Smog repair if it fails: $200–$1,500+ (cat/O2/EVAP); cert itself ~$30–$60
smog_ca_needed.verify_question: Has it failed smog, or just not been tested yet? Failure often means cat or sensor work.

registration_ca.cost_estimate: Back-registration/renewal + late penalties: $150–$700+; non-op cars may need smog too
registration_ca.verify_question: Why is it non-op / unregistered — sitting unused, or a problem preventing registration?

ac_cooling_tx.cost_estimate: AC recharge $50–$150; compressor/condenser $500–$1,500 (critical in TX/AZ heat)
ac_cooling_tx.verify_question: Is it just low on freon (cheap recharge) or a dead compressor (expensive)?
english: ac blows but no cold air

positive_inspection_tx.verify_question: How recent is the inspection sticker?

inspection_needed_tx.cost_estimate: Inspection + any failed-item repairs; emissions repair varies
inspection_needed_tx.verify_question: Did it fail inspection, or just hasn't been done?

engine_caution.cost_estimate: Overheating: thermostat $150 / head gasket $1,000–$2,500. Oil use: top-off vs ring wear.
engine_caution.verify_question: Overheating — does it hold temp on a test drive? Oil use — how much per 1,000 miles?

diesel_emissions.cost_estimate: Deleted diesel fails inspection; DPF/EGR restore $1,500–$4,000; emissions tamper = legal risk
diesel_emissions.verify_question: Is it 'deleted'? That fails emissions inspection and can be a legal/registration problem.

water_damage_concealment.cost_estimate: Flood/water cars = chronic electrical + mold; often a total loss long-term. Treat low-priced clean-title outliers with suspicion.
water_damage_concealment.verify_question: Any musty smell, water lines, or electrical gremlins? Pull a title-history report.

positive_emissions_az.verify_question: Is the emissions test current (AZ requires it for registration in Maricopa/Pima)?

emissions_needed_az.cost_estimate: Emissions repair if it fails: cat/O2/EVAP $200–$1,500; test ~$25
emissions_needed_az.verify_question: Did it fail emissions or just not been tested? Failure often = cat or sensor.

rust_structural.cost_estimate: Structural — frame/subframe rust or damage kills resale & can fail safety; often uneconomical to fix
rust_structural.verify_question: Is the rust structural (frame, subframe, rockers, floor) or just surface? Get it on a lift.

rust_cosmetic.cost_estimate: Cosmetic only — typical for salt-belt cars; minimal impact if not structural
rust_cosmetic.verify_question: None needed if confined to surface/body panels.

positive_rust_free.cost_estimate: None — rust-free is a premium in the salt belt
positive_rust_free.verify_question: Confirm underbody in photos — 'rust free' claims vary.

cosmetic_dents_hail.cost_estimate: Cosmetic — hail/door dings don't affect mechanics; PDR optional ($75–$150/dent)
cosmetic_dents_hail.verify_question: None mechanically; only matters for your resale photos.

fresh_paint_concealment.cost_estimate: Paint itself is fine; risk is WHAT it hides — accident repair, rust, or bondo underneath
fresh_paint_concealment.verify_question: Why was it repainted? One panel = likely accident/rust repair. Check panel gaps & overspray.

open_recall.cost_estimate: Recall fix usually free at dealer — but confirm it's been done; open safety recalls matter
open_recall.verify_question: Is the recall open or completed? Check VIN on the NHTSA recall site.

fuel_leak_red.cost_estimate: Fuel/gas leak = fire hazard; do not drive until fixed
fuel_leak_red.verify_question: Where is it leaking — line, tank, or injector seal? This is a safety stop.

history_serious.verify_question: Is it a lemon-law buyback or manufacturer buyback? Pull the title brand history.

engine_caution_extra.cost_estimate: Varies: lifter/vacuum cheap-moderate; sludge/stalling can be serious
engine_caution_extra.verify_question: Does it stall/hard-start when warm or cold? Any ticking that changes with RPM?

drivetrain_caution.cost_estimate: Clutch $800–$1,500; torque converter shudder; u-joint/driveshaft moderate
drivetrain_caution.verify_question: Manual: does the clutch slip under load? Auto: any shudder at light throttle?

cooling_system.cost_estimate: Heater core $600–$1,000 (labor); fan $150–$400; ignore = overheating risk
cooling_system.verify_question: Does it hold temperature on a 20-min drive? Does the cooling fan kick on?

suspension_steering.cost_estimate: Ball joints/tie rods $150–$400 ea (safety); struts vary; clunks = worn parts
suspension_steering.verify_question: Any clunk over bumps or play in the wheel? Ball joints/tie rods are safety items.

brakes_caution.verify_question: Soft/spongy pedal or grinding? That's beyond normal pad wear — inspect calipers/lines.

fuel_system.verify_question: Does it sputter or die at low tank? Fuel pump and injector issues mimic each other.

exhaust_system.cost_estimate: Exhaust leak/hanger cheap-moderate; loud = leak or missing parts
exhaust_system.verify_question: Is it loud from a leak (cheap weld) or a missing cat (expensive)?

electrical_caution.cost_estimate: Gremlins can be cheap (fuse) or costly/elusive (wiring, draw)
electrical_caution.verify_question: Is it one circuit or random? Parasitic drains and wiring faults take diagnosis time.

odometer_discrepancy.cost_estimate: Odometer/title mileage mismatch — possible rollback or branded title. Value drops sharply; verify title brand & Carfax before any offer.
odometer_discrepancy.verify_question: Why does the title/record mileage differ from the dash? Is the title branded TMU/'not actual miles'?

contradictions._description: When 2 keywords from different categories appear, one signals the other is suspect

rules.message: Description claims clean title but mentions accident/structural damage. Title may be misrepresented.
Description contradicts itself — major mechanical issues mentioned alongside positive claims.
Clean title but mileage discrepancy — Carfax verification essential.
Claims one owner but engine was swapped — original engine likely failed.

negation_logic._description: When these words appear BEFORE a problem keyword (within 7 words), the problem is RESOLVED or NEGATED

examples.input: no malfunction except the ABS sensor
examples.result: PARTIAL NEGATION → YELLOW for ABS only

examples.input: transmission slipping but i had it rebuilt
engine was swapped, 60k on new engine

filters._description: Things to SKIP when parsing seller description
facebook_templates: Hello How are you? Im interested Whats your best price?
Hello how are you? I'm interested. What's your best price?

minimal_description.message: Limited information from seller. Ask about title, accidents, maintenance history, and recent repairs before going to see it.
minimal_description.spanish_message: Información limitada del vendedor. Pregunta sobre el título, accidentes, historial de mantenimiento.

templates.title: RED FLAG — Major Issue Detected
CAUTION — Verify Before Buying
Description looks clean — Photo check recommended
templates.body: No major issues mentioned, but the deal is unusually strong ({margin}). Check photos carefully for hidden damage (panels, frame, paint mismatch).

templates.title: Positive signals detected
templates.body:
Seller mentions: {positives}

These are good signs for a flip. Verify with paperwork.

templates.title: Dealer Ad — Not a Private Flip Target
templates.body: This listing uses dealer language (financing, ITIN, down payments). It's a dealership selling retail, not a private seller flip opportunity. Skip.

templates.title: Potential Scam Pattern Detected
templates.body:
Description contains scam markers: {scam_phrases}

Do NOT send money. Do NOT engage. Meet in person at a public location only.

templates.title: Very Limited Info
templates.body: Seller wrote less than 30 words. Before going, ask: title status, any accidents, maintenance history, recent repairs.

templates.title: Verify mileage on detail page
templates.body: This listing's front card has no mileage. Open the full listing to confirm odometer reading before judging the deal — a missing mileage can hide a high-odometer car.
templates.spanish_body: Este anuncio no muestra el millaje. Abre el anuncio completo para confirmar el odómetro antes de evaluar el precio.

_changelog.v1.0: Initial library. Synthesized from Claude + ChatGPT + DeepSeek + Gemini + Meta analyses + 80 real listings from NJ/NY Facebook Marketplace.
_changelog.v1.1: Added California signals from 109 LA-area listings (77 with seller descriptions): new categories positive_smog_ca (POSITIVE), smog_ca_needed (YELLOW), registration_ca (YELLOW); Spanish salvage/clean-title variants + common typos (clean tittle, título linpio, titulo salve); CA pink-slip & lien-sale terminology; Spanish 'por partes'; extended clean-title contradiction with Spanish triggers; added INFO mileage-verify popup. CEL still always YELLOW (Mete's rule, unchanged).
_changelog.v1.2: Added Texas signals from 98 listings (87 with descriptions). NOTE: this TX sample was urban sedans — predicted hail/flood/diesel-truck vocabulary did NOT appear (target DFW=hail, Houston=flood, rural=diesel for those). New categories: ac_cooling_tx (YELLOW — AC critical in TX/AZ heat), positive_inspection_tx (POSITIVE), inspection_needed_tx (YELLOW), engine_caution (YELLOW — folds in Meta-batch validation gaps 'overheating' & 'oil consumption'), diesel_emissions (YELLOW — forward coverage for truck batches). Added cold-AC positives + Spanish, 'clean texas title'. CEL still always YELLOW.
_changelog.v1.3: Houston add (file grew 98->132 listings, +34 Houston-area). FINDING: flood/storm/hail vocabulary still 0 — sellers conceal flood damage, it does not appear as free-text keywords. Only real gaps added: tow-required RED variants (must tow / tow only / won't drive); new water_damage_concealment YELLOW for the INDIRECT tells (musty, mold, mildew, electrical gremlins, water line). Flood detection should be a STRUCTURAL heuristic (price<<model value + salvage/rebuilt + coastal + thin description), not keyword matching.
_changelog.v1.4: Phoenix AZ add (132 listings, 82 with descriptions, UNFILTERED collection per Mete's method correction). FINDING: predicted 'no rust / desert car' vocabulary did NOT appear (0 hits) — sellers don't romanticize it the way northern buyers do. Real AZ signal: AC dominant (51x, > TX), heavy Spanish (47x), emissions. Added positive_emissions_az (POSITIVE) + emissions_needed_az (YELLOW) — AZ's smog/inspection equivalent; sun/cosmetic desert wear -> normal_wear_ignore GREEN (sun-faded paint, cracked dash = do NOT flag); Spanish 'título salvaje' + 'restored salvage' -> title_critical RED; carfax positives. CEL still YELLOW.
_changelog.v1.5: Detroit MI add (128 listings, 85 with descriptions). The genuine gap this fills: RUST — library had ZERO rust coverage because all prior regions (NJ/CA/TX/AZ) were rust-light. Detroit had rust 15x vs ~0 in sun-belt. Added rust_structural (RED — frame/subframe/rusted-out, value-killing & safety), rust_cosmetic (GREEN — surface/minor rust = normal salt-belt wear, do NOT flag, per Mete's philosophy), positive_rust_free (POSITIVE — premium in salt belt). NOTE bare 'rust' intentionally NOT a keyword (would misfire on 'rust free'). Confirms climate+language thesis: Detroit AC dropped 51->15, Spanish 47->3 (cold/English-dominant). CEL still YELLOW.
_changelog.v1.6: Oklahoma City add (117 listings, 80 with descriptions). HAIL PREDICTION FAILED A 2ND TIME (after TX): only 1 hail mention in 117 listings, and the seller framed it 'just cosmetic'. 'storm/tornado/wind' hits were ~all false positives ('windows/windshield'). CONFIRMED RULE: weather-damage vocabulary (hail/flood/storm) does NOT appear in seller free-text regardless of region — it's a structural-heuristic problem, not keywords. Only real add: cosmetic_dents_hail (GREEN — hail/door dings = cosmetic, do NOT flag, matches seller framing) + 'freeon' typo. Low yield, as expected for a non-extreme climate. CEL still YELLOW.
_changelog.v1.7: Miami FL add (110 listings, 74 with descriptions). Heaviest Spanish market yet (density 71, > AZ's 47). PREDICTIONS MISSED: 'no inspection' framing 0 (sellers don't advertise the ABSENCE of a requirement), Caribbean slang 0, coastal corrosion 0 — confirming core automotive Spanish is shared across dialects, as flagged. Genuine yield was a UNIVERSAL (not Miami-specific) concept the library was missing: fresh_paint_concealment (YELLOW — recent/partial repaint can hide accident/rust/bondo) + EN & ES forms; plus Caribbean 'camina' positive. Confirms diminishing returns: more regions now mostly re-confirm climate(AC)+language(ES). CEL still YELLOW.
_changelog.v1.8: New Orleans LA add (110 listings, 76 with descriptions). REGIONAL PREDICTIONS FAILED COMPLETELY: humidity/mold/mildew 0, coastal/salt-air corrosion 0, flood/Katrina 0 — NOLA's warm-humid 'story' produced no new regional vocabulary (AC 23, Spanish 28, standard mechanical issues only). BUT it surfaced genuine UNIVERSAL gaps the library was missing: blue smoke (oil burning, YELLOW — vs existing white-smoke RED), misfire/rough idle, cv axle, transmission leak, and vague-noise descriptors (crackling/popping/grinding/whining/clunking). Added to engine_critical RED (white smoke generalized) + engine_caution YELLOW. These aren't NOLA-specific; NOLA just happened to expose them. Strongest saturation signal yet: regions now add ~0 regional vocab. CEL still YELLOW.
_changelog.v1.9: Seattle WA add (only 35 listings, 29 with descriptions — a partial sample). PNW regional predictions FAILED: moss/moisture 0, no-salt/no-rust 0, no-emissions 0. Cool climate showed as ABSENCE of AC (2 vs Phoenix 51) — validates climate model, adds no keywords. Like NOLA, value was UNIVERSAL mid-tier mechanical gaps the library lacked (it was strong on dramatic failures, weak on common mid-cost issues): standalone head gasket, timing belt/chain, catalytic converter (incl. theft — very common), wheel bearing, water pump, coolant leak, power steering, AWD limp. Split RED (needs head gasket, timing chain rattle, cat stolen, AWD fault) vs YELLOW (head gasket mention, timing belt due, wheel bearing, water pump, coolant/PS leaks). RECOMMENDATION: do NOT complete Seattle to 100 — regional vein is empty and 35 listings already surfaced the universal gaps. Library saturated at 1054 keywords. CEL still YELLOW.
_changelog.v1.10: Seattle completed to 97 listings (71 with descriptions, up from 35). PNW regional veins STILL 0 at full size (moss/no-salt/no-emissions) — definitively not a sample-size issue. Expanded set surfaced more UNIVERSAL gaps: valve cover/oil pan gasket & oil leak (GREEN, minor per Mete's rule), rear main seal/alternator/starter/control arm/O2 & knock sensors/thermostat/radiator/serpentine belt (YELLOW), hybrid battery failure (RED), open_recall (new YELLOW category), and many maintenance/replaced POSITIVES (fresh oil change, alternator/battery/plugs replaced). Library now broad across dramatic + mid-tier + maintenance-positive. CEL still YELLOW.
_changelog.v2.0: SYSTEMATIC CLOSURE PASS (no new region). Checked library against a full used-car taxonomy by system and filled the mid-tier gaps the regional sampling missed (it was strong on dramatic failures, blank on whole systems). New categories: fuel_leak_red (RED, fire safety), history_serious (RED — lemon/buyback/unibody), engine_caution_extra, drivetrain_caution (clutch/torque/u-joint), cooling_system (heater core/fan), suspension_steering (ball joint/tie rod/clunks), brakes_caution (caliper/master cyl/spongy), fuel_system (pump/injector), exhaust_system, electrical_caution. Added accident history -> YELLOW. GREEN normal-wear expanded per Mete's rule (struts/shocks/alignment/brake pads/rotors/bald tires/fuel filter/muffler/fuses = do NOT flag). CEL still YELLOW. Library now systematically complete across all major vehicle systems.

term_costs.power steering leak: Power steering: pump/hose $200–$700; whine = low fluid or pump
term_costs.evap leak: EVAP leak often a $20 gas cap to $300 purge valve
term_costs.evap code: EVAP code often a $20 gas cap to $300 purge valve
term_costs.cv axle clicking: CV axle $150–$400 each; clicking = worn joint
term_costs.axle clicking: CV axle $150–$400 each; clicking = worn joint
term_costs.clicking when turning: CV axle $150–$400; clicking when turning = worn joint
term_costs.losing coolant: Coolant loss: hose/pump/gasket — get pressure test
term_costs.catalytic converter code: Cat code: sensor first ($200) before cat ($400–$2,500)
term_costs.timing belt: Timing belt service $500–$1,100 (do water pump too)
term_costs.needs timing belt: Timing belt service $500–$1,100 (do water pump too)
term_costs.timing belt due: Timing belt service $500–$1,100 (do water pump too)
